subtopics.title: Definition and Branches of Art
subtopics.teaching_note: Art = creative expression of human imagination and skill. Branches: Visual arts (drawing, painting, sculpture, printmaking, photography), Performing arts (music, dance, drama), Liberal arts (literature, philosophy, history). Functions of art: communication, expression, decoration, preservation of culture, education, economic (tourism, export). Nigerian art: Nok terracotta (500 BCE - 200 CE), Ife bronze heads, Benin bronzes, Igbo Ukwu bronzes, contemporary artists (Ben Enwonwu, Nike Davies-Okundaye, El Anatsui).

subtopics.title: The Elements of Art
common_misconceptions: Form and shape are the same (shape is 2D, form is 3D)
There are only three primary colours (for light: RGB; for pigment: RYB)
Texture can only be felt (can be implied visually)
subtopics.teaching_note: Line: mark with length and direction. Types: horizontal, vertical, diagonal, curved, zigzag. Shape: 2D enclosed area (geometric, organic). Form: 3D object (cube, sphere, cylinder). Colour: hue, value (tint/shade), intensity. Colour wheel: primary (red, yellow, blue), secondary (orange, green, purple), tertiary. Warm colours: red, orange, yellow (advance). Cool colours: blue, green, purple (recede). Value: lightness/darkness. Texture: actual (tactile) or implied (visual). Space: positive (filled) and negative (empty).
